author: crible-improver-3 (eco runbook, single-author panel — flagged by design)

dimensionScores.rationale: Core screen/compute path correct & tested (181 pytest+62 vitest green, reconcile aligns periods, NULL never imputed), but the FIRST eval of the newly-landed EDGAR/ESEF/stooq pipeline surfaces real logic bugs: F9 ESEF _fiscal_year tags by end-year with no full-year check (interim→annual, esef.py:87-103), F10 concept-collision last-writer-wins (esef.py:71), F1 run_loop resets the rate budget each cycle (service.py:788,178). No P0.

Broad FR-tagged suite green incl. test_refresh sequencing, but every P1 found (F1/F2/F9/F11) passes the suite — the edge paths of the new pipeline are uncovered; run_loop is # pragma: no cover (TODO P4).

Injection impossible by construction (DSL whitelist+placeholders), bulk zip read in-memory + bootstrap is_relative_to guard, httpx timeouts on edgar/esef/stooq; minor: F12 stooq solve_pow unbounded PoW = CPU DoS on hostile difficulty (stooq_fetch.py:61-68).

Clean module seams, but F4: ingest/service.py grew 370→820 LOC nesting 14 (worst hotspot) as the EDGAR/ESEF/bulk cycles landed in one file, GLEIF/connection/heartbeat boilerplate duplicated (service.py:223-234 vs 507-518) — a real regression vs last cycle.

Read path benchmarked p95<1s (NFR-008) and ranks write-side; pipeline scaling gaps: F3 ranks frame fragmentation, F7 full snapshot rebuild every cycle (no incremental), F13 bootstrap buffers the whole dataset in RAM (OOM on a small host).

topFindings: F6/F5 (bulk-first quick wins: GLEIF auto-fetch, universe refresh)

author: crible-improver-3 (eco runbook, single-author panel — flagged by design)

dimensionScores.rationale: Default shipped path (yfinance-only) is correct and tested; the confirmed defects sit in the loop and the opt-in audited layer. TODO gaps adjudicated in code: watchdog (F2) and universe-refresh-in-loop (F5) CONFIRMED real; GLEIF auto-fetch CONFIRMED absent (F6, dead URL constant gleif.py:21); incremental compute (F7) and FX (F8) confirmed missing.

37 test files, property-based DSL, NFR-008 benchmark, EDGAR/ESEF fixture-driven cycles; gap is the pipeline edge cases (interim ESEF duration, .tmp glob, budget across cycles) none of which have a red test.
Keyless design intact, no traversal in bulk zip handling, secrets absent; the residual risks are DoS/robustness (F12 PoW, F2 hang) not confidentiality.
Provider seam and layer boundaries are clean; the single blemish is the 820-LOC service.py god-module (F4) that every new data source will have to edit.
Fast read path; self-hosting perf frictions dominate: full recompute each cycle (F7, seam exists via build_snapshot(symbols=)), bootstrap full-buffer download (F13), double HF parquet scan.

topFindings: F6 (GLEIF auto-fetch unlocks the idle audited-EU layer)

author: crible-improver-3 (eco runbook, single-author panel — flagged by design)

dimensionScores.rationale: No wrong answer on the default path; but two docstrings (prices.py:6-7, yfinance_provider.py:6) claim a crawler watchdog that does not exist (F2) and run_refresh's own docstring warns of the exact budget bust run_loop commits (F1) — comment/reality contradictions that reliability leans on.
Green and broad, but the reliability-critical loop path is # pragma: no cover and the found defects are all test-invisible.
Strong by-construction posture; F12 unbounded PoW is the one exploitable-ish DoS on the automated stooq path (manual archive path is the safe fallback).
F4 hotspot doubled — the module mixing queue/budget/ESEF/EDGAR/prices/compute/heartbeat is where the next regression will hide.
Read SLA held; write/scale/self-host perf (F3/F7/F13) are the open items, none blocking today at POC scale.
